sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

Invitation Homes is a premier single-family home leasing and management company and an S&P 500 company.

feed.title: US taps Chubb for $20bn maritime insurance backstop
feed.summary: The United States has launched a $20 billion maritime reinsurance facility, led by the US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) and underwritten by Chubb Ltd, to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative aims to provide insurance protection for vessels and cargo, insuring losses up to $20 billion on a rolling basis, thereby boosting market confidence amidst escalating geopolitical risks and attacks in the region. Both DFC and Chubb executives emphasized the critical need for this coverage to resume energy and commercial trade, especially as marine insurance markets face significant exposure estimated by Moody's to be as high as $40 billion.

feed.title: Holed Star Bulk bulker is not listing and is able to sail under its own power
feed.summary: A Star Bulk bulker that sustained a hole is reportedly not listing and retains the ability to sail under its own power. Additionally, another Star Bulk vessel in the same region has been confirmed safe. This news comes amidst tensions related to the Iran-Israel conflict.

feed.title: Digital Convergence Technologies (DCT) Joins Akamai Partner Connect Program
feed.summary: Digital Convergence Technologies (DCT) has joined the Akamai Partner Connect Program, aiming to simplify complex IT challenges for mid-market and enterprise clients. This collaboration focuses on modernizing legacy architectures and enabling cost-effective cloud modernization, particularly for customers in media, healthcare, and fintech. The partnership combines Akamai’s global infrastructure with DCT’s refactoring expertise to create scalable and efficient cloud solutions.

feed.title: Regulators approve We Energies purchase of 150-megawatt solar project for data center
feed.summary: The Wisconsin Public Service Commission has unanimously approved We Energies' acquisition of the 200-megawatt Dawn Harvest Solar and Battery project. This project, located in Rock County and built by Invenergy, will provide 150 megawatts of solar energy and 50 megawatts of battery storage to potentially serve a data center campus in Port Washington. The total cost for the project is estimated at $443 million.
